title: Why was Asuka more friendly toward Rei than with Shinji
display_title: Why was Asuka more friendly toward Rei than with Shinji
wikitext:
[]: Why was Asuka more friendly toward Rei than with Shinji             Quote Post by TheMente » Sat Mar 22, 2014 7:07 pm in their first met? When she met Shinji (episode 8), she didn't really care for him. But she wanted to become friend with Rei in episode 9, who was also a pilot and a possible "rival". I thought she maybe found Rei cuter than Shinji, but that would make her even more hostile, no?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Mar 22, 2014 7:31 pm Rei is a qualified and trained pilot, the very first person selected to be an Eva pilot in fact(hence the "First Children" codename), so she migh be closer to Asuka's standards, in contrast to Shinji who's essentially a rookie and doesn't have a warrior mentality and confidence she expects from Eva pilots(even though he has the highest Angel killcount) Also, there were surrounded by their schoolmates, so Asuka had her sweet facade on. Needless to say, that sweet facade didn't lasted long in front of Rei's "weirdness" which made Asuka even more aggravated by her than with Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Mar 22, 2014 7:34 pm I'm with El on this one. In Asuka's eyes, Shinji has no business being where he is. Even though Rei is competition, being a pilot that actually seems to know what she's doing, Asuka would hold her in higher esteem.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Mar 23, 2014 7:22 pm Asuka wasn't friendly AT ALL with Rei. The condescension is almot palpable, she rudely asks Shinji, "Where is the other one ", positions herself at a higher place to make sure she is looking down on Rei... she doesn't even really go and talk toher, but just blocks her light and expects Rei to start the talking for her because that's how awesome Asuka believes herself to be, adresses her as 'the pilot of the pototype', and goes to randomly demand adoration from a random stranger. When she gets a 'meh' in response, she takes it personally. *shakes head* If you look at that scene and arrive at the conclusion that Asuka was in any way interested in genuine friendship, or anything other than her ego/ illusion of perfection, you clearly weren't paying attention. With Shinji, she was introducing herself in just about the same boisterous way, untill this unlucky misshap with the wind exposing her panties happened, and things went downhill from there due to Touji's... extreme lack of diplomacy, and everyone else kind of standing around him; There's really no reason to interpret anything deeper into it: Rei didn't get to see her panties.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Wed Mar 26, 2014 9:21 am The correct answer is that she treated them both like equally like crap.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 26, 2014 10:15 am Kendrix wrote: If you look at that scene and arrive at the conclusion that Asuka was in any way interested in genuine friendship, or anything other than her ego/ illusion of perfection, you clearly weren't paying attention.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Jive » Sat May 03, 2014 3:10 am To be fair Rei nore Shinji didn't try to be friends whit Asuka either. Sure she a pain but you have to reach out at some point seeing how you gonna have to work together. But when you have one who stay at a rundown apartment from the rest of the world, while another listen to his earphone whit his back against the world, leading to the 3 not bonding well at all. You can't just fault one person for the fail relationship of the Three.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun May 04, 2014 11:58 am Mr. Jive wrote: To be fair Rei nore Shinji didn't try to be friends whit Asuka either.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun May 04, 2014 12:37 pm Kendrix: As someone who shares many of Rei's traits I'll note here that things don't work out the way you describe in practice. It's easy to say "I just want to be left alone" or "I'm not bothering anyone, don't get on my case" but other people tend to take that as rejection, or an implied sense of superiority, or something similar. How the person actually feels doesn't enter into it -- what matters is that they're not like everyone else, and so cause problems just by being there and failing to act as expected. The first time this happens it amounts to an error, and the person is blameless. But if the person sticks to their attitude and doesn't "get with the program", so to speak, they bear some portion of the blame since they're aware of the problem and have failed to take steps to correct it. Yes, that's a twisted way of doing things, and it sucks for the introverts among us (far moreso if you're avoidant or, heaven forbit, schizoid), but the world is what it is. At some point you have to learn to get along with others and properly socialize if you don't wanna be ostracized. Also, I'll mention again that Asuka was cooking for Shinji as well. They took turns, as with all of the other chores. We already know he did it more often -- and that started with Misato, not Asuka, btw -- but it was not a one way street. She even notes his concern in ep 22 - and all it does is piss her off. She wants and needs help, but at the same time, absultely hates being helped and feels it would mean that she is somehow weak and pathetic. Wanting mutually exclusive things tends to make life complicated.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun May 04, 2014 12:47 pm Except Asuka was the only one who perceived it that way. Everyone else was perfectly fine with ignoring her back and while they thought she was weird and seemingly uninterested in further socializing, no one acted like she was "rejecting" them or being haughty. Absolutely no-one . To say "the world is how it is" in such contexts is nothing less than to sanction bullying. Maybe it (like racism, ableism and other such maladies of society) will not ever magically go away, or at least, the impulses that lead people to start it, but there's places where it happens less than others and that's those who have zero tolerance to people who feel it's right to cause suffering to others simply because they don't understand them.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun May 04, 2014 1:14 pm Kendrix wrote: Except Asuka was the only one who perceived it that way.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Mon May 05, 2014 2:43 am Bagheera wrote: You're missing the point. I'm telling you that's how it works in real life. I know this from first hand experience. And people did perceive it that way in the show, that's why she was labeled creepy or weird to begin with.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon May 05, 2014 6:34 am BC Baron wrote: Just my opinion, but perception is NOT reality. Anyone who tells you different is probably not very perceptive or has a rather poor grasp of reality. Possibly both.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon May 05, 2014 7:04 am Can't we just agree that it was both girls' fault if they couldn't get along, Rei for being too detached and Asuka for being too aggressive?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon May 05, 2014 7:29 am ElMariachi wrote: Can't we just agree that it was both girls' fault if they couldn't get along, Rei for being too detached and Asuka for being too aggressive?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Mon May 05, 2014 8:27 am ^I agree. Besides, there's no chance they could have had a normal friendship even if one of them was totally free from issues. Hikari, the most normal of the teens, fails to open Asuka up at all and none of the cast ever succeeds in changing Rei's demeanour even slightly. So it's not like the communication problems would just go away if one of them had fewer or no issues.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon May 05, 2014 2:30 pm Bagheera wrote: It amounts to "don't be a dick", which applies both to not picking on people and to giving people the proper respect when they're trying to interact with you.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon May 05, 2014 3:02 pm NemZ wrote: Maybe so, but in this particular case I can't see Asuka's attempt at "being friends" as actually a friendly act.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon May 05, 2014 3:24 pm NemZ wrote: Maybe so, but in this particular case I can't see Asuka's attempt at "being friends" as actually a friendly act. The way she goes about it, from starting off with the intentionally annoying shadow games to her offer clearly being intended as a public act of 'charity', and by bringing a whole crowd along with her to put Rei utterly on the spot and isolated all come across to me as the more subtle forms of bullying common among girls. Specifically, she's putting herself literally on a pedestal by belittling and socially isolating a rival so that whether Rei accepts or declines she ends up gaining social capital.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon May 05, 2014 3:28 pm Chuckman wrote: The most common weapon they use on each other is peer pressure and the threat of embarassment or negative attention.
